title: GIX M1 stream scenario
description: Synthetic-data stream config consumed by the harness (workstream B) via `npm run stream -- --scenario <path>`. Matches docs/mvp-m1-integration-contract.md §B: order rate, #providers, #consumers, qty/price distributions, fault-injection rates.

name.description: Scenario id; surfaced in the run summary.

seed.description: RNG seed for reproducible synthetic streams.

orderRatePerMin.description: Target consumer order arrival rate (orders/min); Poisson if the harness models arrivals stochastically.

providers.description: Number of simulated provider accounts to stake + mint + post asks.

consumers.description: Number of simulated consumer accounts placing orders.

markets.description: Market names to trade in; must correspond to markets present in deployment.json (matched by .markets[].name). If a name is absent on-chain the harness should skip or warn.

price.description: Limit/market price distribution, in USDC per SCU. Used to size bids/asks and escrow.

faults.description: Per-job fault-injection probabilities (0..1). Each maps to a lifecycle fault the harness induces so the corresponding slash/refund path fires.

skipAttest.description: Provider never submits attestation -> MISSING attestation -> 100% bond-share slash (B4).

lateAttest.description: Attestation arrives after the SLA/attest deadline -> SLA breach -> graded 10-50% slash (B4).

wrongOutput.description: Attested output_hash/measurement is wrong -> INVALID attestation -> 100% bond share + flat penalty (B4).

consumerAbandon.description: Consumer locks escrow then abandons -> D2 forfeit (~10%) + refund.

honestFraction.description: Fraction of providers that never intentionally fault (faults still apply per-job to the rest).

latencyJitter.description: Relative jitter applied to the per-market latency profile (see examples/fixtures/latency-profiles.json).

autoExpireOverdue.description: Call expire_and_resolve on jobs past deadline.

settleDelaySec.description: Artificial delay before submitting attestation, to model execution time.
